title: Future Continuous
definition: The Future Continuous tense describes an action that will be in progress at a specific time in the future.

structure.positive: I will be traveling this time tomorrow. / She will be attending the conference.
structure.negative: I will not (won't) be traveling tomorrow.
structure.question: Will you be traveling tomorrow?
structure.whQuestion: What will you be doing at 8 PM tomorrow?

usage: Use Future Continuous for an action that will be ongoing at a specific future time (This time next week, I will be sitting on a beach), and for polite questions about someone's plans (Will you be using the car tonight?).
examples: This time tomorrow, I will be flying to Delhi.
She will be attending a meeting at 10 AM.
They will be waiting for us at the airport.
I will not be working next Sunday.
Will you be joining us for dinner tonight?
At 9 PM, we will be watching the match.
What will you be doing this time next year?

dailyConversation.speaker: A
dailyConversation.line: Will you be free at 6 PM?

dailyConversation.speaker: B
dailyConversation.line: No, I will be attending a client call at that time.

dailyConversation.speaker: A
dailyConversation.line: What will you be doing this weekend?

dailyConversation.speaker: B
dailyConversation.line: I will be traveling to my hometown.

commonMistakes.wrong: She will being working at 5 PM.
commonMistakes.right: She will be working at 5 PM.
commonMistakes.why: Use 'be', not 'being', in the Future Continuous formula.

quiz.question: At 8 PM, we ___ (watch) the match.
quiz.explanation: Action in progress at a specific future time.

quiz.question: Which sentence best fits Future Continuous?
options: This time tomorrow, I will be flying to Delhi.
quiz.answer: This time tomorrow, I will be flying to Delhi.
quiz.explanation: Describes an action in progress at a specific future moment.
